structure.notes: All required Stage 0 roles are present and reviewable. The locked block at lines 155-177 correctly identifies a PartialID tuple, assumptions are labelled, and the Formalization checklist at lines 605-618 is actionable as a math-object list.

novelty.notes: The derivation is real but appears subfield-level, below novelty_target=field. The response-contribution formula is a useful hand-indexed sharp specialization of standard finite response-type LP machinery, and the product-envelope counterexample is valuable, but the note does not yet establish a field-level new PartialID principle or broad compatibility characterization.

correctness.notes: The main response-contribution algebra, product-envelope counterexample, and coarsened-equation arithmetic check out. The remaining correctness gap is Proposition \ref{prop:strict-aggregation}: the single-law strict witness is valid, but the claimed open-set persistence relies on a compressed semicontinuity argument without a stated parametric LP continuity lemma or explicit neighborhood construction.

perItemFindings.label: Locked parameters, lines 155-177
perItemFindings.one_line: The note correctly treats the anchor as PartialID with tuple (qid, specialization, P, theta, restrictions, bounding functional), not as a panel note.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem \ref{thm:sharp-memory}
perItemFindings.one_line: The closed-form interval is useful, but as written it is a narrow two-stage one-sided IV specialization of standard finite latent-response sharp-bound machinery, yielding assessed tier=subfield below novelty_target=field.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem \ref{thm:product-fails}
perItemFindings.one_line: The product-envelope failure is a valid correction to the Stage -1 conjecture, but it is a compatibility counterexample rather than a field-level identification theorem.

perItemFindings.label: Proposition \ref{prop:strict-aggregation}
perItemFindings.one_line: The displayed strict witness is arithmetically correct, but the proof that strict containment persists on an open set needs a concrete LP continuity/neighborhood lemma.

perItemFindings.label: Counterexample or minimality check, lines 545-603
perItemFindings.one_line: The product endpoint and coarsened equations check numerically: J_N=[1/10,3/5], J_M=[1/4,3/4], product u_1=1, memory upper endpoint 9/10, and coarsened endpoints 0 and 1 are feasible.

perItemFindings.label: Related work and positioning, lines 84-92
perItemFindings.one_line: The literature review is targeted and honest, but it positions the result as a hand-indexed reduction next to Balke-Pearl/Autobounds-style machinery rather than isolating a field-tier advance.

verbatim_critique: Structure passes. The note has all required Stage 0 sections, labelled assumptions, a cluster-aware use of the inherited Regression estimator section, and a usable Formalization checklist. Correctness is substantially improved relative to the earlier stored review: the coarsened feasible set is now explicitly an image relaxation A B_e(a,b)=A T(P), avoiding the false collapsed-randomization issue when e_2 depends on discarded Z_1. The response-contribution proof is algebraically sound: the pure never-taker branch identifies nu_N, rho_N, gamma_N; the mixed branch gives the three linear equations; and the remaining freedom in Y(0,1) is exactly the complier nonresponder mass. The product-envelope counterexample also checks: the marginal product can combine m_1=3/4 and n_1=1/2 to produce u_1=1, while the common latent completion has upper endpoint 2/5+1/2=9/10. The coarsened strict-gain witness now uses the correct weighted equations A=1/12 and B=1/4, and the endpoint completions for targets 0 and 1 satisfy 0<=g_s<=r_s<=1. The remaining correctness repair is local but real: Proposition \ref{prop:strict-aggregation} claims strict containment on an open set, but the proof only invokes upper/lower semicontinuity of finite LP endpoints. Add a precise parametric LP continuity/stability lemma with maintained feasibility and positive slack, or weaken the proposition to the displayed strict witness. The larger obstacle is novelty. The derivation appears tier=subfield below novelty_target=field. The main theorem is a clean analytic formula for this special two-stage one-sided IV design, and the product-envelope failure is a useful warning about incompatible marginal projections, but both sit inside standard finite response-type partial-identification/LP machinery. To meet the field floor, the note would need a genuinely broader compatibility characterization, a general memory-gain theorem, or a nontrivial class of dynamic IV designs for which the response-contribution reduction opens a regime not already implicit in Balke-Pearl/Autobounds-style sharp-bound machinery.
